questions.question_text: What is the highest permitted blood alcohol level when driving a vehicle?
The car ahead is travelling at about 40 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at 50 km/h and are approaching a built-up area and a bus. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do here?
You want to continue straight ahead. What do you do now?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You are driving at about 50 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have reduced speed to about 20 km/h and moved your foot to the brake before the difficult bend. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 50 km/h. What do you do?
You are driving at 40 km/h and the road is very slippery. What do you do?
You are driving at 50 km/h and have caught up with the very slowly moving car ahead. What do you do?
You have caught up with the cyclists. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h and must continue straight ahead. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You want to turn left as soon as possible. What do you do here?
You are driving at 30 to 40 km/h and must continue straight ahead. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have reduced your speed to 30 km/h in order to turn right. What do you do?
What should you pay particular attention to a little further ahead?
You are driving on a motorway. What should you pay particular attention to a little further ahead?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You are driving at about 60 km/h and are about to enter the motorway. What do you do?
You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.

questions.question_text: You have stopped and want to move off from the kerb. What do you do?
You are driving at about 20 to 30 km/h, the same speed as the motorcyclist ahead. You must continue straight ahead on a road with a bend. What do you do?
You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are prohibited here.

questions.question_text: You have got out of the red car to run an errand. Which rule applies here?

answers.2: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
